title: Introduce daemon architecture; restructure milestones v3-v5
decision: Retire the planned v3 Intelligence milestone. Introduce v3 Daemon (client-server split with standalone daemon process), v4 Flow II (deferred client UX enhancements), and v5 Intelligence (rebuilt on daemon). All PTY management moves to daemon; Electron client becomes a pure display/input layer that connects to N daemons via WebSocket + TLS.

context.task_description: User testing revealed that the primary workflow involves running AI agents on remote VMs via SSH, and working from multiple workstations. The current local-only PTY architecture cannot support session mobility or remote session management without manual SSH workarounds.
assumptions: Developer works across multiple machines (lab workstation + laptop over Wireguard)
Sessions run on VMs in a lab environment, not on the desktop
Network interruptions occur (WiFi drops on laptop) and sessions must survive them
Full scrollback history replay is needed after reconnect

options.label: Daemon-first architecture
options.description: Introduce a standalone daemon for PTY management. Client connects via WebSocket + TLS. All sessions daemon-managed including localhost. Intelligence features deferred to v5, built on daemon.

options.label: Keep local-only; add SSH tunneling
options.description: Keep the current monolithic architecture. Add SSH-based PTY forwarding to support remote sessions. Session mobility solved via session state export/import.

options.label: Interleave daemon and intelligence
options.description: Build daemon and intelligence features concurrently. Rejected because intelligence features depend on knowing where the PTY output stream lives — building them on the current architecture and then migrating to daemon would require rework.

rationale: The daemon is foundational infrastructure. Status inference, session timeline, and the plugin API all need the PTY output stream, which moves to the daemon. Building intelligence on the current local-PTY model and then migrating would mean building it twice. Daemon-first means intelligence features become easier to implement, not harder. Client-side UX features (queued prompts, tray, templates, groups) are independent and can be built in parallel.

risk.rollback: If daemon architecture proves infeasible, the existing v1+v2 local-only architecture remains fully functional. Planning artifacts can be reverted.

outcome.notes: Architecture defined in NORTHSTAR.md. Sprint planning for v3 Daemon pending.
